records.ety: [OF. durance duration, fr. L. durans, -antis, p. pr. durare to endure, last. See Dure, and cf. Durant.]

quotes: Of how short durance was this new-made state!

defs: Imprisonment; restraint of the person; custody by a jailer; duress. Shak.
quotes: In durance, exile, Bedlam or the mint.

defs: A stout cloth stuff, formerly made in imitation of buff leather and used for garments; a sort of tammy or everlasting.
In modern manufacture, a worsted of one color used for window blinds and similar purposes.
quotes: Where didst thou buy this buff? let me not live but I will give thee a good suit of durance.
